preamble: The voiceless retroflex fricative /ʂ/ underwent a significant transformation to the voiceless dental fricative /θ/ in the historical development of Castilian Spanish. This sound change represents a fronting of the articulation point within the mouth.

examples.note: This was a phonemic shift that occurred in the sibilant system of Hispano-Romance, specifically the fronting of the retroflex fricative to a dental fricative.

sources: Dworkin, S. N. (2018). A Guide to Old Spanish. Oxford University Press.
